content.code:
std::stringstream os {};
os << "en garde!\n"; // insert "en garde!" into the stringstream

list: Use the str(string) function to set the value of the buffer:

content.code:
std::stringstream os {};
os.str("en garde!"); // set the stringstream buffer to "en garde!"

list: Use the str() function to retrieve the results of the buffer:

content.text: clear() resets any error flags that may have been set and returns the stream back to the ok state.
